content_markdown:
The ratio

$$\frac{\text{thermal capacity of a sample of copper}}{\text{specific heat capacity of copper}}$$

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present, only text and a mathematical fraction.

vibe_explanation: Thermal capacity ($C$) is defined as $C = mc$, where $m$ is the mass and $c$ is the specific heat capacity. Therefore, the ratio of thermal capacity to specific heat capacity is $\frac{C}{c} = \frac{mc}{c} = m$. Since the result of the ratio is mass, the unit must be kilograms (kg).
